page: Amazon Web Services t2.2xlarge is a burstable performance instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor. It features 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores running at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Xen hypervisor, does not include local storage, and lacks dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with some limitations in high-throughput scenarios. The single-core stress-ng performance score is 1737.48, and the multi-core score is 11556.72, both falling within the average range. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is measured at 625.85 GB/sec, though large-block write bandwidth drops to a weak 24.83 GB/sec. The instance demonstrates poor performance in single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 7.12 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 0.87 million operations per second, reflecting its burstable CPU allocation model.

This instance offers a cost-effective solution for workloads with variable CPU utilization. Because it relies on a burstable CPU credit model, it is highly efficient for applications that remain idle or run at low baseline levels but require occasional bursts of full CPU performance. It is well-suited for development environments, code compilation, low-traffic web servers, and small database operations that do not demand sustained high-throughput compute or storage performance.
description: Amazon Web Services t2.2xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Operating under the Xen hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 11556.72 and a Passmark CPU score of 10905.03. However, it exhibits weaker performance in high-throughput tasks, such as large-block memory writes and single-threaded decompression. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads with variable CPU demands, making it suitable for software development, code compilation, and low-traffic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t2.2xlarge is a burstable instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, suitable for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t2.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A burstable x86_64 instance with balanced memory and CPU resources designed for workloads with variable performance demands.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB RAM
Burstable CPU allocation model for variable workload demands
Xen hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suitable for development environments, code compilation, and low-traffic web servers
